fallacy_name: Lean 4 Syntax and Logic Failure (Unresolved Broken Cut-and-Paste Formalization)
attack_vector: The Lean 4 code in the second block still contains the exact same broken formalization of the Cut-and-Paste lemma that was pointed out in the previous turn. `cut_paste_property` is defined as `compute_crossing x1 y1 = compute_crossing x2 y2 \rightarrow output_of_pair x1 y1 = output_of_pair x2 y2`, which merely states that the crossing sequence determines the output. It completely misses the mixed-input splicing property `output_of_pair x1 y2 = output_of_pair x1 y1`. Consequently, the proof of `FRSB_CutAndPaste_Crossing_Sequence_Bound` fails with a type mismatch. When you `apply cut_paste_property (x1 := a) (y1 := a) (x2 := a) (y2 := b)`, the generated goal is `compute_crossing a a = compute_crossing a b`. However, you attempt to close this goal with `exact h_cross_eq`, which has the type `compute_crossing a a = compute_crossing b b`. This is a blatant type error and the code will not compile.
director_pivot_advice: You successfully fixed the first block, but you forgot to update the second block! You must update `cut_paste_property` in the second block to match the correct Cut-and-Paste definition: `compute_crossing x1 y1 = compute_crossing x2 y2 \rightarrow output_of_pair x1 y2 = output_of_pair x1 y1`. Then, in the proof, apply it correctly so that the generated premise exactly matches `h_cross_eq` (`compute_crossing a a = compute_crossing b b`). Do not leave broken code in one block while fixing the other.
